campaign_brief.objective: Establish LSAD as the trusted low-SWaP autonomous detection system for tactical edge environments, securing 5 qualified program-of-record evaluations and 2 pilot contracts within the first two government fiscal quarters of launch.
campaign_brief.target_audience: Defense acquisition program managers, C5ISR systems integrators, and DoD R&D labs evaluating next-generation autonomous sensing and threat detection hardware for contested environments.
campaign_brief.key_message: LSAD delivers ITAR-compliant, low-size-weight-and-power autonomous detection at the tactical edge — operating reliably in GPS-denied, comms-degraded conditions where legacy systems fail.
campaign_brief.proof_point: Validated in DoD-relevant field trials with sub-50ms detection latency, MIL-STD-810H environmental qualification, and operation at under 12W power draw — a 40% SWaP reduction versus incumbent systems.
campaign_brief.call_to_action: Request a classified-environment capability briefing and schedule a hands-on evaluation unit deployment.

pain_points: Incumbent detection systems exceed SWaP budgets on dismounted and small-UAS platforms
Pressure to field capabilities faster under accelerated acquisition pathways (OTAs, MTAs)
Need ITAR/EAR compliance and a clear path to authority-to-operate (ATO) for cyber
Risk-averse to unproven vendors lacking past performance or TRL maturity
information_sources: Defense News, Breaking Defense, and National Defense Magazine
AUSA, SOFIC, and Sea-Air-Space trade shows
DSIAC/DTIC technical reports and SBIR/STTR solicitation databases
Trusted systems integrator and prime contractor recommendations

buying_committee.their_question: Does this reduce program risk and meet my SWaP and schedule requirements?
buying_committee.content_needed: TRL maturity brief, past performance summary, acquisition pathway fit sheet

buying_committee.their_question: Will it integrate with our existing C5ISR architecture and meet MIL-STD specs?
buying_committee.content_needed: Interface control document, environmental qualification test data, MOSA/SOSA alignment whitepaper

buying_committee.their_question: Is the vendor compliant, vetted, and contractually viable (CMMC, ITAR, DFARS)?
buying_committee.content_needed: Compliance attestation packet, CAGE code, CMMC 2.0 status documentation

buying_committee.their_question: Will this actually work in the field and reduce my cognitive/physical load?
buying_committee.content_needed: Field trial video, operator workload assessment, ruggedization demo

icp.company_size: Defense primes (10,000+ employees), mid-tier integrators (500-5,000), and government program offices
geographies: United States (CONUS DoD installations and acquisition hubs)
Five Eyes allied nations (UK, Australia, Canada)

messaging_hierarchy.headline: Detect Threats Where Legacy Systems Go Blind
messaging_hierarchy.subheadline: LSAD brings autonomous, low-power detection to the contested tactical edge — GPS-denied, comms-degraded, mission-ready.
messaging_hierarchy.body_message: Built for the warfighter and the program manager alike, LSAD delivers sub-50ms autonomous threat detection at under 12W power draw, fully MIL-STD-810H qualified and ITAR-compliant. Its modular, SOSA-aligned architecture integrates into existing C5ISR systems without a forklift upgrade, cutting integration risk and accelerating your path to fielding under modern acquisition pathways.
messaging_hierarchy.tone: Authoritative, technically credible, mission-focused, and free of marketing hyperbole

channels.channel: Defense trade shows & live demonstrations (AUSA, SOFIC, Sea-Air-Space)
channels.rationale: Face-to-face hands-on demos are the dominant trust-building channel in defense procurement; decision-makers and integrators concentrate here.
channels.kpi: Number of qualified evaluation unit requests generated

channels.channel: Direct ABM outreach to program offices & primes
channels.rationale: Defense buying is relationship- and committee-driven; targeted account-based engagement reaches named PMs and integrators directly.
channels.kpi: Number of capability briefings booked with target accounts

channels.channel: Defense trade media & thought leadership (Breaking Defense, National Defense)
channels.rationale: Builds credibility and TRL/past-performance perception among the persona's primary information sources.
channels.kpi: Earned/sponsored placements and content engagement from .mil/.gov and prime domains

channels.channel: LinkedIn targeted campaigns (defense acquisition & engineering titles)
channels.rationale: Cost-efficient air cover to nurture the buying committee and reinforce messaging between in-person touchpoints.
channels.kpi: Engagement rate and content downloads from targeted defense audience

channel_recommendation.channel_sequence: Lead with trade media thought leadership to seed credibility 6 weeks pre-launch, activate ABM outreach to book briefings, peak at a flagship trade show with live demos during launch week, then sustain momentum with LinkedIn nurture and follow-on evaluation deployments.

allocation.channel: Direct ABM outreach to program offices & primes

content_creative_plan.hero_asset: A field-trial capability film showing LSAD operating in a GPS-denied, comms-degraded scenario, with overlaid telemetry proving sub-50ms latency and sub-12W power draw, plus a paired technical capability brief.

supporting_assets.purpose: Establish TRL maturity and SOSA/MOSA architecture alignment for chief engineers
supporting_assets.specs: 12-page PDF, controlled-distribution version available, diagrams of interface control

supporting_assets.purpose: Map LSAD to OTA/MTA/SBIR acquisition pathways for program managers
supporting_assets.specs: Single-page PDF, plain-language, compliance and CAGE code footer

supporting_assets.purpose: Hands-on evaluation experience at trade shows and on-base briefings
supporting_assets.specs: Ruggedized portable LSAD unit with scenario playback rig and ATAK integration

supporting_assets.purpose: Reassure contracting officers on ITAR, DFARS, and CMMC 2.0 readiness
supporting_assets.specs: Bundled PDF with attestations and certification status

content_calendar.action: Publish thought-leadership op-ed in Breaking Defense on tactical-edge SWaP challenges
Launch ABM email + call sequence to top 25 target program offices
Release hero field-trial film (public + controlled versions)
Begin LinkedIn campaign targeting acquisition and engineering titles
Distribute technical whitepaper to engaged technical authorities
Pre-show outreach to schedule on-floor demos at flagship trade show
Flagship trade show live demonstrations (launch week)
Post-show follow-up, deliver evaluation units, publish recap content

actions: Finalize compliance packet (ITAR, DFARS, CMMC 2.0 status)
Seed trade media thought leadership and secure show floor demo slot
Build and validate ABM target list of named PMs and integrators
Produce hero film and controlled-distribution technical brief
Run live demonstrations on show floor and at scheduled private briefings
Release hero asset publicly and issue launch press placement
Capture qualified evaluation-unit requests and book follow-on capability briefings
Activate LinkedIn campaign to amplify show presence
Deploy evaluation units to top qualified accounts and support trials
Nurture buying committee with role-specific content (chief engineer, CO, end user)
Convert evaluations into pilot contracts via acquisition-pathway support
Report on pipeline and iterate messaging based on objection patterns

launch_timeline.defense_timing_note: Align the launch and pilot pushes with the US Government fiscal year, which runs October 1 to September 30. Aim evaluation deployments for Q1-Q2 FY (Oct-Mar) so pilots can mature into funded line items ahead of the Q4 (July-September) 'use it or lose it' obligation surge, and pre-position for the President's Budget release (typically February) to influence next-year program funding.

sales_enablement.one_pager_summary: LSAD is an ITAR-compliant, SWaP-optimized autonomous detection system that delivers sub-50ms threat detection at under 12W in GPS-denied, comms-degraded tactical environments. MIL-STD-810H qualified and SOSA-aligned, it integrates into existing C5ISR architectures without a forklift upgrade, reducing integration risk and accelerating fielding under OTA, MTA, and SBIR pathways.
talk_track: Open with the operational gap: legacy detection fails where it matters most — GPS-denied, comms-degraded edge environments.
Quantify the LSAD advantage: 40% SWaP reduction, sub-50ms latency, under 12W power, MIL-STD-810H qualified.
De-risk the decision: emphasize TRL maturity, SOSA/MOSA alignment, and ITAR/DFARS/CMMC compliance.
Map to the acquisition pathway and propose a concrete next step: a no-cost evaluation unit deployment in their environment.

objection_handling.objection: You don't have established past performance with our program office.
objection_handling.response: Understood — that's exactly why we offer a no-cost, in-environment evaluation unit. You validate performance against your requirements before any contractual commitment, and we provide field-trial data and reference contacts from comparable DoD evaluations.

objection_handling.objection: We're concerned about integration risk with our existing C5ISR architecture.
objection_handling.response: LSAD is SOSA/MOSA-aligned with published interface control documents and ATAK integration out of the box, so it slots into your architecture without a forklift upgrade. Our engineers will co-develop the integration plan with your chief engineer during the evaluation.

objection_handling.objection: Procurement and ATO timelines make new vendors hard to onboard.
objection_handling.response: We map LSAD directly to accelerated pathways like OTAs, MTAs, and SBIR Phase III, and we arrive with a complete compliance packet — CAGE code, ITAR, DFARS, and CMMC 2.0 status — to shorten contracting and cyber review cycles.

demo_sequence: Stage a GPS-denied, comms-degraded scenario and power on LSAD to show autonomous boot and detection
Display live telemetry proving sub-50ms latency and sub-12W power draw
Demonstrate ATAK / C5ISR integration passing detection data to the operator interface
Run a ruggedization/environmental stress vignette to confirm MIL-STD-810H field readiness
Close with the evaluation-unit deployment offer and acquisition pathway mapping

kpis.primary_kpi: Number of qualified pilot/evaluation contracts secured within first two government fiscal quarters

channel_kpis.target: 25 requests across the launch trade show

channel_kpis.channel: Direct ABM outreach to program offices & primes
channel_kpis.kpi: Capability briefings booked with target accounts
Earned/sponsored placements engaging .mil/.gov and prime audiences
Content downloads from targeted defense audience

kpis.reporting_cadence: Weekly pipeline pulse during launch window, full campaign performance review monthly with quarterly executive readout

risks.risk: Procurement and ATO cycles delay evaluations beyond the campaign window
risks.mitigation: Pre-stage compliance documentation and pursue accelerated pathways (OTA/MTA/SBIR Phase III); offer no-cost evaluation units that bypass long contracting cycles for initial validation.

risks.risk: Lack of established past performance stalls trust with risk-averse program offices
risks.mitigation: Lead with field-trial data, third-party test validation, and reference contacts; partner with an established prime as a subcontractor to inherit credibility and contract vehicles.

risks.risk: Federal budget continuing resolution freezes new-start program funding
risks.mitigation: Position LSAD against existing programs of record and reprogramming-eligible budgets; time pilot pushes to align with appropriations resolution and the President's Budget release.

pivot_triggers: Fewer than 10 qualified evaluation requests after the launch trade show — shift budget from media to direct ABM and one-on-one base briefings
Continuing resolution extends past Q2 FY — pivot messaging toward allied Five Eyes opportunities and SBIR-funded pilots
Integration objections dominate sales conversations — accelerate prime/integrator partnership strategy over direct program-office selling
